source: SquidSec community vulnerability database (local JSON). Extend freely.

vulnerabilities.title: Outdated WordPress core (example threshold)
vulnerabilities.description: Very old WordPress core versions have numerous known issues.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Update WordPress to the latest secure release.

vulnerabilities.title: TimThumb arbitrary file upload / RCE lineage
vulnerabilities.description: Legacy TimThumb is unsafe and should not exist on modern sites.

vulnerabilities.title: File Manager family — high risk if unpatched
vulnerabilities.description: Historical unauthenticated RCE in File Manager plugin lineage. Treat any install as high scrutiny.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Remove if unused; otherwise keep strictly updated and restrict access.

vulnerabilities.title: Legacy Slider Revolution issues (broad advisory)
vulnerabilities.description: Older Slider Revolution versions had critical issues.

vulnerabilities.title: Very old Contact Form 7
vulnerabilities.description: Outdated contact form plugins frequently accumulate XSS/file issues.
